title: Obica
content:

{{Quote|Located in the western reaches of Grumani sector, Obica isn't considered particularly strategic by any of the known parties at odds in the region. But the world is noteworthy as the holy world of the Spumani, a site of annual pilgrimages and seemingly semi-annual wars.|A cartographical survey and field report by Vannar Treece|<ref name="Gazetter" />}}
'''Obica''' was an [[Outer Rim Territories/Legends|Outer Rim Territories]] [[Planet/Legends|planet]] located in the [[Grumani sector/Legends|Grumani sector]]. The holy world of the [[Spumani]] religion, it was the site of regular pilgrimages and even more frequent wars. At one point, access to the planet was controlled by the Spumani-affiliated [[Scholastic Guardians]]; [[Standard century/Legends|centuries]] later, during the [[New Sith Wars]], the [[Galactic Republic/Legends|Galactic Republic]] was no longer aware of the state of affairs on Obica. When the [[Alliance to Restore the Republic/Legends|Rebel Alliance]] was preparing for the [[Battle of Endor/Legends|Battle of Endor]] of [[4 ABY/Legends|4 ABY]], the [[Sullustan Home Guard/Legends|Sullustan Home Guard]] [[Officer/Legends|officer]] [[Syub Snunb]] rendezvoused with the [[Rebel Command Fleet]] near Obica, from where he then led it to the [[Sullust system/Legends|Sullust system]].

==Description==
{{Quote|Tanta Aurek derives its name from the fact that it and Tanta Besh appear to be a double star in the skies of Obica, though the two systems are actually far apart.|A cartographical survey and field report by Vannar Treece|<ref name="Gazetter" />}}
Obica was a terrestrial<ref name="Gazetter" /> [[Planet/Legends|planet]]<ref name="SWE">''[[Star Wars Encyclopedia]]''</ref> situated in the [[Obica system/Legends|Obica system]],<ref name="EAOC" /> within the western reaches of<ref name="Gazetter" /> the [[Grumani sector/Legends|Grumani sector]]<ref name="EAOC" /> that lay in the [[Trailing Sectors/Legends|Trailing Sectors]] portion of<ref name="TrailingSectors" /> the [[Outer Rim Territories/Legends|Outer Rim Territories]].<ref name="EAOC" /> The [[Tanta Aurek system/Legends|Tanta Aurek]] and [[Tanta Besh system/Legends|Tanta Besh systems]] appeared to be a double [[Star/Legends|star]] from the [[Atmosphere/Legends|skies]] of Obica, leading to the former's naming.<ref name="Gazetter" />

==History==
{{Quote|Centuries ago, access to Obica was controlled by the feared Scholastic Guardians, who would interrogate visitors to ensure they were members of the Spumani faithful.|A cartographical survey and field report by Vannar Treece|<ref name="Gazetter" />}}
At one point, the group known as the [[Scholastic Guardians]], who had a fearsome reputation, controlled access to Obica and subjected any visitors to interrogation in order to ensure that they were faithful members of the [[Spumani]], a breakaway branch of<ref name="Gazetter" /> the [[Children of Mani]] religion.<ref name="FarHorizons">''[[Far Horizons]]''</ref> [[Standard century/Legends|Centuries]] later,<ref name="Gazetter" /> by [[1032 BBY/Legends|1032 BBY]],<ref name="1032 BBY">{{SW|url=news/essential-atlas-extra-the-knight-errant-gazetter|text=Essential Atlas Extra: The Knight Errant Gazetteer|int=The Knight Errant Gazetteer}} is presented as an [[in-universe]] [[Astrography|cartographical]] survey and field report compiled shortly before the [[Raid on Oranessan|commencement]] of [[Operation Influx]], which {{SWArchive|url=vault/books/knighterrant_influx/index.html|text=''Star Wars: Knight Errant: Influx''|int=Star Wars: Knight Errant: Influx}} dates to [[1032 BBY/Legends|1032 BBY]].</ref> [[Jedi Master/Legends|Jedi Master]] [[Vannar Treece]] noted in a [[Astrography|cartographical]] survey and field report on the Grumani [[Sector/Legends|sector]] that the [[Galactic Republic/Legends|Galactic Republic]] was not aware of the current situation on Obica, although<ref name="Gazetter" /> he<ref name="Influx">{{SWArchive|url=vault/books/knighterrant_influx/index.html|text=''Star Wars: Knight Errant: Influx''|int=Star Wars: Knight Errant: Influx}}</ref> also commented that none of the sector's conflicting parties of the [[New Sith Wars]] at the time considered the world to be of particular strategic importance.<ref name="Gazetter" />

[[File:SyubSnunb-SWR.png|thumb|right|300px|Syub Snunb rendezvoused with the Rebel Command Fleet near Obica to lead it to the Sullust system.]]
During the preparation of the [[Alliance to Restore the Republic/Legends|Rebel Alliance]] [[Battle of Endor/Legends|assault]] on the [[Galactic Empire/Legends|Galactic Empire's]] [[Death Star II/Legends|second Death Star]] [[Death Star/Legends|battle station]]<ref name="DES">''[[Dark Empire Sourcebook]]''</ref> in [[4 ABY/Legends|4 ABY]],<ref name="NEC">''[[The Essential Atlas]]''</ref> the entire [[Alliance Fleet/Legends|Alliance Fleet]] was to assemble in the [[Sullust system/Legends|Sullust system]]<ref name="DES" /> of the Grumani sector's neighboring [[Brema sector/Legends|Brema sector]].<ref name="OuterRim">{{SW|url=news/essential-atlas-outer-rim-sectors|text=The Essential Atlas Extra: Outer Rim Sectors|int=Star Wars: The Essential Atlas Online Companion}}</ref> [[Syub Snunb]], an [[Officer/Legends|officer]] of the [[Sullustan Home Guard/Legends|Sullustan Home Guard]] [[Fleet/Legends|fleet]], was selected to lead the [[Rebel Command Fleet]] to the Sullust [[Star system/Legends|system]]<ref name="DES" /> and to that end rendezvoused with the Rebel leaders at a point near Obica.<ref name="CSWE">{{CSWECite|II|402|Obica}}</ref> There, the Rebels thoroughly checked Snunb out, and, when they were satisfied that they were not walking into a trap, the Alliance forces followed the [[Sullustan/Legends|Sullustan]] officer to the planet [[Sullust/Legends|Sullust]].<ref name="DES" />

==Inhabitants==
Obica was notable for being the holy world of the Spumani. It was visited annually by pilgrims and saw the occurrence of wars that, according to Vannar Treece, apparently took place twice as often as the pilgrimages.<ref name="Gazetter" />

==Behind the scenes==
Obica was first mentioned in the ''[[Dark Empire Sourcebook]]'', [[Michael Allen Horne]]'s [[1993]] sourcebook published by [[West End Games]] for use with ''[[Star Wars: The Roleplaying Game]]''.<ref name="DES" /> The [[2009]] reference book ''[[The Essential Atlas]]'' placed the Obica system, and therefore the planet itself, in grid square M-17.<ref name="TEA" />
